title: Geoeconomics: 'All dependencies, minerals, currencies and semiconductors, are now being weaponized'
subhead: According to geopolitics expert Marc-Antoine Eyl-Mazzega, the competition for control over minerals, logistics and the flow of raw materials is benefiting the US and China. Europe lags behind in this geoeconomic race, with a tangible impact on how major powers are reshaping their defense capabilities.

title: Behind the cordial facade, the Trump-Xi summit yields no significant progress
The rebuilding of Iran's ballistic arsenal challenges claims of a decisive US victory
Thirty-six countries approve creation of special tribunal for Ukraine to try Russian invasion
subhead: Thirty-four of the Council of Europe's 46 members approved a resolution on the tribunal, along with Australia, Costa Rica and the EU. Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky signed an accord with the Council to create the legal body in 2025.

title: Trump claims 'fantastic trade deals' made with China
subhead: The US president did not spell out the trade agreements that he said had been sealed with China. But in a Fox interview, he said one big business deal struck involved Xi agreeing to purchase '200 big' Boeing jets.

title: Cannes 2026: Park Chan-wook, jury president, says he is ready for 'fierce debates'
subhead: The elegant and discreet Korean filmmaker has retained from his years of opposition to dictatorship a taste for rebellion and debate.

title: 'People are hiding out at home, some don't even go to work anymore': Residents of El Paso, Texas try to evade ICE agents
subhead: Long present in this border city with Mexico, where 80% of the population is Hispanic, the US immigration enforcement agency has recently stepped up its operations and arrests. It has even opened a 5,000-bed detention center in the neighboring military zone.

How China is cracking down on its Christians
subhead: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

title: The shattered lives of Iranians Manije, Mohammad-Reza and Soroush, all killed by bombings
subhead: Before the Israeli-US bombings began, they were living as retirees and artists in different parts of Tehran. Until they met their deaths in their own homes. Tormented by distress, their loved ones abroad and in Iran recount their last conversations before fate took a tragic turn.

title: 'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
subhead: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

title: Lessons from Covid-19 in the era of hantavirus
'Israel's impunity in Gaza can only encourage it to be just as ruthless in Lebanon'
Sandra Laugier, philosopher: 'In France as in the US, never has freedom of expression been so often invoked by its enemies'
